body:
if we run:
``` bash
meltano config target-bigquery set overwrite true
```
it will show:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/83bf873e-63d1-44d6-b920-38b3fee85a3f)

if we run
```bash
export TARGET_BIGQUERY_OVERWRITE=true
```
or
in the .env file we put TARGET_BIGQUERY_OVERWRITE=true
will show:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f2f8a178-b29d-49fe-ae9f-56415bd35449)


the string value 'true' of  overwrite will cause a error

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3e824fa7-88aa-426f-9624-860d25c68c2c)

the only way I found to set it right is in the meltano.yml file: overwrite: true


not sure if I did it iwrong, or is there some other way to set it programmly?
title: can not set 'overwrite' to a valid value by interactive mode, ./env file or envieroment variable
